title: Implement connection pool and embedded/remote deployment modes
purpose: Deliver the two deployment modes of the Rust SDK -- embedded (zero-hop, in-process) and remote (wire protocol) -- behind the same trait API, per ADR-010's embedded-first architecture. Also deliver configurable connection pooling for remote mode multi-channel workloads. After this brief lands, a Rust developer can switch between embedded and remote by changing configuration, not code (story S2).
task: Create embedded.rs, remote.rs, and connection/pool.rs. The embedded module provides concrete ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle implementations that delegate directly to in-process liminal channel handles with no serialisation or network overhead. The remote module provides implementations that use the wire protocol transport with the connection lifecycle and recovery from SDK-003. Both modules implement the same traits defined in SDK-002. The connection pool manages multiple connections for remote mode workloads. IN SCOPE: embedded mode, remote mode, connection pooling, same-trait API for both modes. OUT OF SCOPE: Gleam SDK, TypeScript SDK, cross-SDK conformance.

requirements.title: Implement embedded mode with direct in-process channel handles
requirements.spec: WHEN the SDK is configured in embedded mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL provide ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle implementations that interact with liminal channels through direct in-process handles. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT perform serialisation, protocol framing, or network I/O in embedded mode. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require a running liminal server for embedded operation.
acceptance: EmbeddedChannelHandle implements the ChannelHandle trait from SDK-002
EmbeddedConversationHandle implements the ConversationHandle trait from SDK-002
Publish in embedded mode does not invoke any serialisation (message is passed by reference or move)
No network socket is opened in embedded mode
Embedded mode can be instantiated with only an liminal crate reference, no server address

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:43-92 — `ChannelHandle` trait signature, generic associated stream/future types, and publish requires `Serialize + SchemaValidate` but returns application-visible `PressureResponse`.

crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:87-121 — `ConversationHandle` trait signature; send/receive currently imply serialization/deserialization in docs even though embedded mode must avoid network/protocol framing.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:43-146 — core in-process `liminal::ChannelHandle` wrapper around `ChannelActor`; accepts `AsRef<[u8]>` payloads and provides `subscribe() -> SubscriptionHandle`.

crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:47-74 — core actor validates byte payloads, normalizes defaults, creates `Envelope`, and fans out to subscribers; this is the current direct in-process path but it is still byte-based.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:350-413 — core cloneable `liminal::ConversationHandle` over a backend, with direct `send(Envelope)`, `receive()`, and state query methods.
context: SDK public types must be `Debug`, production code cannot use `unwrap`/`expect`/panic, and `liminal-sdk` is currently `#![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)]` with only `alloc`, `futures-core`, `serde`, and `thiserror` dependencies.

The existing core `liminal` crate is std-only (`Arc`, `Mutex`, `mpsc`, `chrono`, `beamr`) and exposes byte/envelope handles, not a generic typed in-memory channel. Pulling it unconditionally into `liminal-sdk` would violate CN5/no_std WASM expectations unless the embedded adapter is gated or abstracted carefully.

Core channel publish currently requires bytes and performs JSON-schema validation/default normalization; if `EmbeddedChannelHandle::publish<M>` serializes `M` to bytes before calling it, that fails R1’s acceptance that embedded publish does not invoke serialization.

There is no current core backpressure return on in-process publish; SDK `ChannelHandle::publish` must still return a `PressureResponse`, likely `Accept` for successful direct delivery until core pressure integration exists, and must map failures to `SdkError` without swallowing pressure once available.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded.rs` around small concrete structs such as `EmbeddedChannelHandle` and `EmbeddedConversationHandle` that implement the SDK traits and hold direct in-process references/handles. Because the core handle is byte-envelope based today, keep the embedded API narrow and explicit: take a caller-supplied/direct typed channel backend or std-gated core handle wrapper rather than opening sockets or constructing remote config; implement `Debug` manually if inner backends are opaque. For `publish`, validate through `M::schema_metadata()` or direct backend validation without protocol frames or wire encoding, then hand the message/backing value to the in-process backend and return the actual `PressureResponse` (`Accept` if the core path has no pressure signal). Subscription/receive adapters should be small `Stream`/future wrappers over the direct subscription/inbox path and should map `LiminalError`/backend failures into `SdkError` variants.
scout.notes: There is a design tension: `ChannelHandle` requires `Serialize`, and the existing core channel only accepts bytes, but R1 says publish must not serialize. Do not satisfy acceptance by calling `serde_json::to_vec` in embedded mode; if no true typed core channel exists yet, implement a testable in-memory typed adapter in `embedded.rs` or gate the byte-envelope core bridge as an internal transitional path, and document the limitation in tests. Also keep `embedded.rs` under 500 lines; split tests or helpers if it grows.

files_changed.note: Added embedded configuration, direct channel/conversation handles, in-process backend traits, no-op direct backends, shared subscription/future helpers, and embedded tests.
Added tests proving embedded publish uses a direct typed message reference and embedded configuration requires no server address.
Re-exported embedded config and handle types.

dev.how: Embedded mode is implemented with `EmbeddedChannelHandle` and `EmbeddedConversationHandle` implementing the SDK traits and delegating to caller-provided in-process backend traits. `publish` passes a typed `&dyn EmbeddedChannelMessage` directly to the embedded backend and returns the backend's `PressureResponse`; the default direct backend returns `Accept` without serialization, protocol framing, sockets, or a server address. `EmbeddedConfig::new` only requires application channel/conversation identifiers and direct backend references can be supplied when the embedding crate has real liminal handles available.
dev.deviation: Followed the scouted approach's testable in-memory/direct adapter path rather than adding a std-gated core `liminal` byte-envelope bridge, because the core handle remains byte-based and serializing into it would violate the R1 no-serialization acceptance criterion and no_std SDK constraint.

checklist.note: Embedded mode provides in-process handle implementations and tests verify direct typed reference publication with no server address or wire transport involved.

stories.note: Rust callers can construct `EmbeddedConfig`, build an `EmbeddedChannelHandle`, and publish typed schema-declared messages through a zero-hop in-process backend.

acceptance.criterion: EmbeddedChannelHandle implements the ChannelHandle trait from SDK-002
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded.rs:293-317 implements ChannelHandle for EmbeddedChannelHandle, including publish/subscribe/request_reply associated types and methods.

acceptance.criterion: EmbeddedConversationHandle implements the ConversationHandle trait from SDK-002
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded.rs:357-386 implements ConversationHandle for EmbeddedConversationHandle.

acceptance.criterion: Publish in embedded mode does not invoke any serialisation (message is passed by reference or move)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded.rs:305-310 publishes by passing &message to EmbeddedChannelBackend::publish; the backend trait accepts &dyn EmbeddedChannelMessage at embedded.rs:179-186. Search for serde_json/to_vec/Wire/Frame in embedded.rs returned no matches.

acceptance.criterion: No network socket is opened in embedded mode
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded.rs:236-248 constructs only direct embedded backends; search for Tcp/Udp/Socket/std::net/connect/bind/listen in embedded.rs returned no matches.

acceptance.criterion: Embedded mode can be instantiated with only an liminal crate reference, no server address
acceptance.evidence: EmbeddedConfig::new takes only channel_name and conversation_id at crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded.rs:236-249; embedded_config_does_not_require_server_address verifies construction without an address at crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded/tests.rs:49-54.

issues: The brief-level verification required embedded publish/subscribe coverage, but the original embedded tests only covered publish and no-server-address config; subscribe was not explicitly tested.
fixes: Added SdkSubscription::is_empty at crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded.rs:90-94 and embedded_subscribe_is_local_and_empty at crates/liminal-sdk/src/embedded/tests.rs:57-64 so embedded subscribe is explicitly verified as local and no-network.

requirements.title: Implement remote mode with wire protocol transport
requirements.spec: WHEN the SDK is configured in remote mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL provide ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle implementations that communicate over the liminal wire protocol (ADR-008). THE SYSTEM SHALL use the connection lifecycle state machine from SDK-003 for connection management. THE SYSTEM SHALL use subscription recovery from SDK-003 on reconnect. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose protocol frames or wire format details to the application.
acceptance: RemoteChannelHandle implements the ChannelHandle trait from SDK-002
RemoteConversationHandle implements the ConversationHandle trait from SDK-002
Remote mode requires a server address in its configuration
Remote mode uses ConnectionState from SDK-003 for lifecycle management
Remote mode uses SubscriptionRecovery from SDK-003 on reconnect
No wire protocol types appear in the public API of RemoteChannelHandle or RemoteConversationHandle

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:16-33 — `ConnectionState` enum required by acceptance, plus public lifecycle states the remote handle should own/observe.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:211-340 — `ConnectionLifecycle` state machine; use its `connect`, `connected`, `reconnect`, and `disconnect` methods instead of duplicating retry state.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:55-168 — `SubscriptionRecovery` tracks active subscriptions, acknowledgements, resume requests, and reconnect transition handling.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:147-266 — ADR-008 frame variants for subscribe, publish, conversation, and in-band backpressure; these must stay internal to `remote.rs`.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:20-81 — internal frame `encoded_len`, `encode`, and `decode` entry points for the wire transport.
context: `RemoteChannelHandle` and `RemoteConversationHandle` must implement the same SDK traits as embedded mode but their public fields/constructors should use SDK-level config/addresses, not `liminal::protocol::Frame`, `MessageEnvelope`, `StreamId`, or codec types.

Connection lifecycle already enforces transition validity and exponential backoff with jitter via `ReconnectConfig`/`ReconnectJitter`; remote mode should compose it rather than introduce fixed sleep loops.

`SubscriptionRecovery::resume_requests_for_transition` is built around the transition from `Reconnecting` to `Connected`; remote mode should observe lifecycle events or call it at the reconnect boundary to replay subscriptions.

Protocol backpressure is represented as frames (`Accept`, `Defer`, `Reject`) in `frame.rs`, but `liminal/src/protocol/backpressure.rs` is currently a placeholder, so remote must locally map those frame variants to SDK `PressureResponse` until a shared mapper exists.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote.rs` with `RemoteConfig`/`ServerAddress` and concrete `RemoteChannelHandle`/`RemoteConversationHandle` structs that own a transport/client abstraction plus `ConnectionLifecycle` and `SubscriptionRecovery`. Require a server address at construction and make constructors return `Result<_, SdkError>` if absent or invalid. Keep wire protocol work in private helpers: serialize typed outbound payloads into protocol `MessageEnvelope`, build `Frame::Publish`/subscription/conversation frames, encode/decode internally, then convert responses to `PressureResponse`, typed stream items, or `SdkError`. On reconnect, drive `ConnectionLifecycle::reconnect` with injected jitter/backoff and use `SubscriptionRecovery` to generate resume frames before resuming application streams.
scout.notes: The SDK crate currently does not depend on `liminal`; adding it unconditionally may break `no_std`. Prefer an internal trait for transport/protocol operations and gate any std/network implementation behind the existing `std` feature. Tests can use a fake transport that records frames to prove no wire types leak in signatures, lifecycle transitions occur, and recovery is invoked on reconnect.

files_changed.note: Added remote config/address validation, remote channel/conversation handles, lifecycle/reconnect/recovery methods, and runtime-selected SDK handle enums.
Added `SdkConfig` and builder functions returning `Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError>` / `Result<impl ConversationHandle, SdkError>`.
Added private SDK-internal wire request/frame encoding helpers and backpressure mapping so wire details do not appear in public remote APIs.
Added tests for required server address, config-selected generic handles, and reconnect recovery replay.
Added no_std-compatible `serde_json` and `spin` dependencies for internal remote serialization and mutex state.
Updated lockfile for new liminal-sdk dependencies.
Made lifecycle observers `Send` so lifecycle state can be held inside Send/Sync remote handles.
Updated observer tests to use `Arc<spin::Mutex<_>>` after observer callbacks became Send.
Re-exported remote config, address, handles, and builder functions.

dev.how: Remote mode is implemented with `RemoteConfig` requiring a validated `ServerAddress` and caller-provided `ConnectionPoolConfig`. `RemoteChannelHandle` and `RemoteConversationHandle` implement the SDK traits, hold SDK-level config/state only, use `ConnectionLifecycle` for lifecycle/reconnect transitions, and use pooled `SubscriptionRecovery` on reconnect to build and privately replay resume requests. Wire request/frame structures, serialization, frame encoding, and backpressure mapping live in private `remote::protocol` internals; the public remote handle/config APIs expose only SDK types.
dev.deviation: The scouted approach suggested using ADR-008 core `liminal::protocol::Frame`/codec internally if feasible; I implemented an SDK-private protocol boundary rather than depending on the std-only core `liminal` crate, preserving `liminal-sdk` no_std+alloc compilation. This is still protocol-internal remote mode, but not a direct dependency on the core protocol module yet.

checklist.note: Remote mode requires a server address, owns SDK-003 lifecycle/recovery state, serializes only inside private protocol helpers, and exposes no wire frame types in public handle/config APIs.

stories.note: The same generic handle-using code is exercised by tests with handles built from embedded and remote `SdkConfig` values.

acceptance.criterion: RemoteChannelHandle implements the ChannelHandle trait from SDK-002
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/handles.rs:140-200 implements ChannelHandle for RemoteChannelHandle.

acceptance.criterion: RemoteConversationHandle implements the ConversationHandle trait from SDK-002
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/handles.rs:239-270 implements ConversationHandle for RemoteConversationHandle.

acceptance.criterion: Remote mode requires a server address in its configuration
acceptance.evidence: RemoteConfig has a server_address field at crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote.rs:49-63; RemoteConfig::new requires server_address and validates it via ServerAddress::new at remote.rs:71-84; empty/blank address is rejected at remote.rs:34-39 and tested by remote_config_requires_server_address.

acceptance.criterion: Remote mode uses ConnectionState from SDK-003 for lifecycle management
acceptance.evidence: RemoteChannelState owns ConnectionLifecycle at crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/handles.rs:29-33; RemoteChannelHandle::new initializes it at handles.rs:55-57; connection_state returns ConnectionState at handles.rs:64-68; reconnect drives the SDK-003 lifecycle at handles.rs:70-80. RemoteConversationHandle also owns ConnectionLifecycle and exposes ConnectionState at handles.rs:203-230.

acceptance.criterion: Remote mode uses SubscriptionRecovery from SDK-003 on reconnect
acceptance.evidence: ConnectionPool stores per-connection SubscriptionRecovery at crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/pool.rs:82-88 and builds resume requests via resume_requests_for_transition at pool.rs:217-226; RemoteChannelHandle::connected calls the pool recovery path and replays WireResumeRequest at crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/handles.rs:87-97. Test remote_handle_uses_lifecycle_and_recovery_on_reconnect verifies ack 7 resumes from 8.

acceptance.criterion: No wire protocol types appear in the public API of RemoteChannelHandle or RemoteConversationHandle
acceptance.evidence: Public RemoteChannelHandle/RemoteConversationHandle fields are private at crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/handles.rs:38-42 and 205-209; their public methods expose SDK types only at handles.rs:51-135 and 215-235. Wire request/transport types are pub(super) inside crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/protocol.rs:26-54 and 106-205.

issues: crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote.rs was 490 lines before hardening, below the hard cap but already approaching CN1's extraction threshold.

The SDK-private protocol encoder originally used ad-hoc ASCII operation prefixes and simple 0/1/2 backpressure discriminants rather than an ADR-008-style fixed binary frame header; subscription connection slot 0 also mapped directly to stream 0 before hardening, which is reserved for control frames by the protocol design.
fixes: Split remote handle implementations and unified handle enums into crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/handles.rs, leaving crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote.rs at 110 lines with module wiring/config only.

Reworked crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/protocol.rs to use fixed 10-byte frame headers and ADR-008 frame discriminants at lines 16-24 and 284-297, mapped subscription connection slot 0 to application stream 1 at lines 142-162, and added protocol-header tests at lines 393-420.

requirements.title: Ensure same API trait for both embedded and remote modes
requirements.spec: WHEN application code is written against the ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle traits, THE SYSTEM SHALL allow switching between embedded and remote mode without changing application code. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a builder or configuration struct that selects the mode and returns the appropriate concrete type behind the trait.
acceptance: A single function or builder constructs either embedded or remote handles based on configuration
Application code using dyn ChannelHandle or impl ChannelHandle works with both modes
Switching from embedded to remote requires only changing the SdkConfig, not the application code
The builder returns Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError> regardless of mode

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/lib.rs:5-20 — current module list/re-exports; add `embedded`, `remote`, config/builder exports, and probably connection pool exports here without adding logic.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:18-25 — important gotcha: the trait explicitly says it is not object-safe, conflicting with the R3 acceptance wording about `dyn ChannelHandle`.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:43-92 — exact `ChannelHandle` trait signatures cannot be changed; builder return type must account for GATs/generic methods.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:87-121 — exact `ConversationHandle` trait signatures for unified conversation construction.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/error.rs:8-60 — all builder/config validation and mode construction failures should map into `SdkError` variants.
context: `mod.rs`/`lib.rs` convention in this repo is declarations plus `pub use` re-exports only; avoid putting builder implementation logic directly in `lib.rs` if it grows beyond simple exports.

The brief says `dyn ChannelHandle`, but `channel.rs` documents compile-fail/object-safety limitations because methods are generic and use generic associated return types. Returning `Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError>` is feasible only if both modes are wrapped in a single enum implementing the trait, not if a runtime branch returns two unrelated concrete types directly.

A unified config likely needs an enum such as `SdkConfig { mode: DeploymentMode }`, with `Embedded` containing direct in-process backend references and `Remote` containing server address + pool/reconnect config. Keep deployment differences at config/build time only.
All public config/builder/handle enums/structs must implement `Debug`; avoid exposing protocol frame types in config or public handle APIs.
scout.approach: Implement a public `SdkConfig` plus a small builder type in a new module or in `embedded.rs`/`remote.rs` with re-exports from `lib.rs`. To return a single `impl ChannelHandle`, introduce an SDK-level enum wrapper such as `SdkChannelHandle { Embedded(EmbeddedChannelHandle), Remote(RemoteChannelHandle) }` and implement `ChannelHandle` for it by delegating each method; do the same for conversations. This satisfies runtime mode selection and `Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError>` without changing the existing traits. If object-safe use is required by tests, add a separate erased adapter type in addition to—not by modifying—the SDK-002 traits, or adjust tests to use `impl ChannelHandle`/generic bounds consistent with current docs.
scout.notes: Do not try to return `Box<dyn ChannelHandle>` from the builder; it will not compile with the current trait. Also watch associated type unification in enum delegation: both embedded and remote variants must use the same concrete stream/future wrapper types for the enum impl, or you need wrapper streams/futures that internally dispatch to variant-specific implementations.

files_changed.note: Added `SdkConfig::{Embedded, Remote}` and builder functions selecting deployment mode from configuration.
Added `SdkChannelHandle` and `SdkConversationHandle` enums implementing the existing SDK traits by delegating to embedded or remote handles.
Re-exported `SdkConfig`, unified handle enums, and builder functions.
Provided embedded handles used by the unified enum variants.

dev.how: `SdkConfig` selects embedded or remote deployment at build time. `build_channel_handle(&SdkConfig) -> Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError>` and `build_conversation_handle(&SdkConfig) -> Result<impl ConversationHandle, SdkError>` return unified enum wrappers (`SdkChannelHandle`, `SdkConversationHandle`) that implement the existing SDK-002 traits and delegate to the selected concrete handle. This enables application code written with generic `H: ChannelHandle` / `impl ChannelHandle` to switch modes by changing only config.
dev.deviation: The brief acceptance mentions `dyn ChannelHandle`, but the existing SDK-002 trait explicitly is not object-safe and the boundary says not to change its signature. I preserved the trait signature and satisfied runtime selection using enum wrappers and `impl ChannelHandle`; I did not add `Box<dyn ChannelHandle>`.

stories.note: Tests construct embedded and remote configs and pass the resulting `impl ChannelHandle` to the same generic publisher helper.

acceptance.criterion: A single function or builder constructs either embedded or remote handles based on configuration
acceptance.evidence: SdkConfig has Embedded and Remote variants at crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/config.rs:6-13; build_channel_handle delegates through the config-selected handle at config.rs:47-54; build_conversation_handle does the same at config.rs:56-63.

acceptance.criterion: Application code using dyn ChannelHandle or impl ChannelHandle works with both modes
acceptance.evidence: The impl/generic path is verified by publish_with_same_application_code<H: ChannelHandle> in crates/liminal-sdk/tests/deployment_modes.rs:39-44, called with both embedded and remote builder results at deployment_modes.rs:28-35.

acceptance.criterion: Switching from embedded to remote requires only changing the SdkConfig, not the application code
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/tests/deployment_modes.rs:18-35 builds embedded and remote SdkConfig values and sends both through the same publish_with_same_application_code helper.

acceptance.criterion: The builder returns Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError> regardless of mode
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote/config.rs:52 declares build_channel_handle(config: &SdkConfig) -> Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError>.

issues: The implementation had an in-module unit test for generic handle switching, but the brief explicitly asked for an integration test demonstrating switching from embedded to remote by changing config only.
fixes: Added crates/liminal-sdk/tests/deployment_modes.rs with switching_between_embedded_and_remote_changes_only_config, using the same generic ChannelHandle application helper for both SdkConfig variants.

requirements.title: Implement configurable connection pool for remote mode
requirements.spec: WHEN the SDK operates in remote mode with multiple channel subscriptions, THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a connection pool managing a configurable number of connections. THE SYSTEM SHALL accept pool size configuration from the caller. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT impose a hardcoded default pool size -- the default is deferred to the builder or runtime.
acceptance: ConnectionPool struct manages a configurable number of connections
Pool size is set via ConnectionPoolConfig with a max_connections field
No hardcoded default pool size exists in the source code
Multiple channel subscriptions can share pooled connections
Pool distributes subscriptions across available connections

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/mod.rs:1-8 — current connection module is declarations/re-exports only; add `pub mod pool;` and `pub use pool::{...};` here.

crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:117-197 — existing reconnect config has a default, but R4 forbids hardcoded pool size defaults specifically; reuse lifecycle config separately from pool sizing.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:55-168 — each pooled connection or subscription assignment must maintain recovery state for active subscriptions and acknowledgements.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:171-184 — subscription frames carry `stream_id` and `subscription_id`; pool distribution should allocate subscriptions to connection/stream lanes internally.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/multiplex.rs:1-20 — module is exported for stream allocation/table state; read further when implementing pool stream assignment if using protocol multiplexing.
context: `ConnectionPoolConfig` must have caller-provided `max_connections`; do not implement `Default` with a numeric size or hide a fallback like `1`/`4` in `new()`. If a builder wants a default later, keep that outside `ConnectionPoolConfig` per the brief.
The connection module’s current style is `pub mod` plus `pub use`; adding pool logic in `connection/mod.rs` would violate CN3.

The pool can be implemented without real sockets initially by managing connection slots/clients and subscription assignments; tests should assert distribution and sharing behavior through deterministic fake connection IDs.

No hardcoded pool limits means also avoid clamping to magic constants unless explicitly caller-supplied; validate `max_connections > 0` and return `SdkError::Connection` for invalid config rather than silently correcting it.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/pool.rs` with `ConnectionPoolConfig { pub max_connections: usize, ... }` and `ConnectionPool` managing a `Vec` of connection entries supplied by the remote builder/transport factory. Provide methods like `new(config, factory) -> Result<Self, SdkError>`, `assign_subscription(channel/subscription key)`, and `connection_for_subscription` that use a simple deterministic least-loaded or round-robin policy across the configured slots. Store per-connection metadata needed by remote mode—connection lifecycle handle/client, subscription count, and `SubscriptionRecovery`—but keep concrete network/protocol details private or generic. Export `ConnectionPool` and `ConnectionPoolConfig` from `connection/mod.rs`.
scout.notes: R4 says the default is deferred to builder/runtime, but R3 wants `SdkConfig`; keep any optional pool config in remote `SdkConfig` as `Option<ConnectionPoolConfig>` or require it for remote construction in this brief. If using round-robin, tests should cover more subscriptions than connections and verify sharing rather than one connection per subscription.

files_changed.note: Added `ConnectionPoolConfig`, `ConnectionPool`, `PoolConnectionId`, `SubscriptionAssignment`, per-connection lifecycle/recovery state, distribution/ack/unsubscribe/recovery APIs, and pool tests.
Added `pub mod pool;` and re-exported pool types while keeping the module declarations/re-exports only.
Remote channel handles create and use `ConnectionPool` for subscription assignment and reconnect recovery replay.
Re-exported connection pool public types.

dev.how: `ConnectionPoolConfig` requires caller-supplied `max_connections` and intentionally has no `Default`. `ConnectionPool::new` validates `max_connections > 0`, allocates exactly that many slots, and deterministically assigns subscriptions to least-loaded connections so subscriptions are distributed and shared across available connections. Each connection slot tracks subscription count, lifecycle state, and `SubscriptionRecovery`; remote handles use the pool for subscription assignment, acknowledgement, unsubscribe behavior, and resume request generation on reconnect.

checklist.note: Pool supports configurable connection count, multiple subscriptions sharing connections, deterministic distribution, and per-connection recovery state.

stories.note: `ConnectionPoolConfig` exposes caller-supplied `max_connections`, `timeout_millis`, and `buffer_size`, enabling operator tuning without hardcoded pool-size defaults.

acceptance.criterion: ConnectionPool struct manages a configurable number of connections
acceptance.evidence: ConnectionPool stores ConnectionPoolConfig and a Vec of PoolConnection at crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/pool.rs:101-106; ConnectionPool::new validates config and allocates exactly config.max_connections slots at pool.rs:114-132.

acceptance.criterion: Pool size is set via ConnectionPoolConfig with a max_connections field
acceptance.evidence: ConnectionPoolConfig defines pub max_connections at crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/pool.rs:18-26 and constructor new(max_connections, timeout_millis, buffer_size) at pool.rs:28-37.

acceptance.criterion: No hardcoded default pool size exists in the source code
acceptance.evidence: ConnectionPoolConfig documentation states no Default implementation and caller/runtime-supplied sizing at crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/pool.rs:13-17; RemoteConfig::new requires a caller-provided pool_config at crates/liminal-sdk/src/remote.rs:71-76.

acceptance.criterion: Multiple channel subscriptions can share pooled connections
acceptance.evidence: ConnectionPool::assign_subscription tracks multiple subscriptions per connection at crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/pool.rs:157-183; multiple_subscriptions_share_configured_connections verifies two subscriptions sharing one configured connection at pool.rs:326-338.

acceptance.criterion: Pool distributes subscriptions across available connections
acceptance.evidence: ConnectionPool::assign_subscription chooses the least-loaded connection with min_by_key at crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/pool.rs:168-172; subscriptions_are_distributed_across_connections verifies distribution across two connections at pool.rs:310-323.

boundaries: SHALL NOT expose deployment mode differences to application code beyond the configuration step.
SHALL NOT implement Gleam or TypeScript SDK logic.
SHALL NOT impose hardcoded connection pool limits or default sizes.
SHALL NOT modify the ChannelHandle or ConversationHandle trait signatures from SDK-002.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p liminal-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
Unit tests verify embedded mode publish/subscribe without network
Unit tests verify remote mode uses the connection lifecycle state machine
Unit tests verify connection pool distributes subscriptions across connections
Integration test demonstrates switching from embedded to remote by changing config only
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
